$comment: Indexed char byte memory (D-CSUBSET-CHAR-STRING-VALUE-CODEGEN): runtime-index byte STORE `q[n]=66` (x86 mov [base+idx*1],r8) + indexed byte LOAD `q[n]` (movzx), through (char*)&int writable stack memory. 66 + 65 = 131. x86_64 ONLY — arm64's lea is symbol-form-only, a pre-existing gap blocking all indexed pointer arithmetic (int and char alike), so the indexed forms have no arm64 realization to exercise; the non-indexed char forms cover arm64 in char_value. The optimizedPipelines arm keeps `n` a runtime value (no const-fold of the index).
